introSection: Drako is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Walibi Holland in Biddinghuizen, Flevoland, Netherlands.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened in 1987 at Zygo Park in France, it was named Tarzungle. It lasted until the park's closure in 1991. It reopened at Flevohof the following year, being repainted and renamed to Keverbaan which translates to Beetletrack. It was located in the park's kids section, now known as Walibi Play Land. The ride was rethemed after the 1999 season, as the park transformed into Six Flags Holland by Six Flags, turning the coaster into Road Runner Express. This changed the ride's color scheme again, along with the addition of some thematic rocks in the middle of the ride.

The coaster got another overhaul after Six Flags sold the park, theming it to Wok, one of the park mascots at the time. It became Wok's Waanzin which translates to Wok's Madness. The ride returned to its red colour and a statue of Wok was added to the rockwork in the middle.

In 2011, the ride got relocated to the spot previously occupied by the Flying Dutchman Gold Mine which closed after the 2010 season. This new spot in the Wild Wild West area saw another retheme; it became the Rattlesnake, featuring a new train design and yet another new colour scheme.

The old theater next to the coaster was transformed into Club Psyke in 2012 and the Rattlesnake got another new theme to fit in with this new ride in 2014, being renamed to Drako and receiving another new train design.

Lost Gravity opened on Drako's former spot in 2016, transforming the Wild Wild West area into the Zero Zone. Club Psyke closed in this year, which left drako without any coherency with its theming.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Drako is a Tivoli Medium coaster from Zierer.

fullText:
Drako is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Walibi Holland in Biddinghuizen, Flevoland, Netherlands.

The ride originally opened in 1987 at Zygo Park in France, it was named Tarzungle. It lasted until the park's closure in 1991. It reopened at Flevohof the following year, being repainted and renamed to Keverbaan which translates to Beetletrack. It was located in the park's kids section, now known as Walibi Play Land. The ride was rethemed after the 1999 season, as the park transformed into Six Flags Holland by Six Flags, turning the coaster into Road Runner Express. This changed the ride's color scheme again, along with the addition of some thematic rocks in the middle of the ride.

The coaster got another overhaul after Six Flags sold the park, theming it to Wok, one of the park mascots at the time. It became Wok's Waanzin which translates to Wok's Madness. The ride returned to its red colour and a statue of Wok was added to the rockwork in the middle.

In 2011, the ride got relocated to the spot previously occupied by the Flying Dutchman Gold Mine which closed after the 2010 season. This new spot in the Wild Wild West area saw another retheme; it became the Rattlesnake, featuring a new train design and yet another new colour scheme.

The old theater next to the coaster was transformed into Club Psyke in 2012 and the Rattlesnake got another new theme to fit in with this new ride in 2014, being renamed to Drako and receiving another new train design.

Lost Gravity opened on Drako's former spot in 2016, transforming the Wild Wild West area into the Zero Zone. Club Psyke closed in this year, which left drako without any coherency with its theming.

Drako is a Tivoli Medium coaster from Zierer.
